text:
Market today: No direct tech/chip news, but broader market sentiment seems mixed. ECB holding rates but hinting at hawkishness could create volatility, while strong bank earnings suggest some underlying economic strength. This tug-of-war could impact your growth-oriented tech names.

Your holdings: SOXL is a major concern, down -37.71% and flagged "SELL." SPAL, another leveraged ETF, is also deeply in the red at -48.51% and "NEW." QQQI is on "TRIM" at -2.09%. Remember, trading on margin amplifies these drops significantly.

Watch: Keep a very close eye on the stops for SOXL (200) and SPAL (21.38). These leveraged positions can move fast and decay, making stop discipline critical. Also, monitor QQQI's stop at 52 if you decide to trim.

Bottom line: Focus on risk management, especially with your leveraged positions and existing drawdowns, before considering any new positions.
plays: SOXL: The "SELL" status and -37.71% loss on this leveraged ETF demand attention. Respect the stop at 200 or consider trimming to reduce exposure to decay and volatility.
SPAL: This new, leveraged ETF is down -48.51%. Given its nature and your margin use, strictly adhere to its stop at 21.38 to prevent further significant losses.
QQQI: With a "TRIM" status and -2.09% loss, consider reducing your position if it approaches its stop at 52, especially given the broader market uncertainty.
